meta.setting: Back lots behind French Gulch storefronts
meta.rdr_clone_target: PS3-era RDR-clone western art, grounded in domestic labor, rumor, and back-door pressure

research_grounding.title: NPS Whiskeytown History and Culture
research_grounding.use: Mining-community family, work, and settlement context.

research_grounding.title: California State Parks Shasta State Historic Park
research_grounding.use: Old northern California buildings and town material.

image_prompt.subject: back-lot kitchens behind French Gulch buildings: wash lines, ash barrels, chickens, herb beds, and a child watching riders through fence slats
image_prompt.scene: late afternoon; stove smoke, stacked firewood, muddy footpaths, cooks and washerwomen at work, a side door half-open, no sentimental gloss
image_prompt.composition: square layered domestic exterior with fences and back doors creating secret sightlines; laundry and ash barrels frame the human work
image_prompt.camera: waist-height alley view, quiet open-world side area with readable paths and hiding places
image_prompt.hand_cut_note: Make the lot feel private but not small; whole town politics pass through the back door.

style_modifiers.medium: PS3-era open-world western game art, early-HD in-engine town-back scene
render_traits: slightly simplified figures, chickens, fences, and laundry silhouettes
modest texture resolution on cloth, wood, mud, feathers, and smoke
hard ambient occlusion under fences, basins, ash barrels, and steps
late afternoon light with stove smoke haze
believable 1899 domestic tools and work clothing
lived-in social-pressure composition rather than tidy homestead scene
style_modifiers.palette: laundry white, ash gray, mud umber, stove smoke blue, firewood brown, garden green, faded calico, and warm low sun.

text_rendering.instructions: No readable signs, labels, notices, or text.

negative_prompt: copy of commercial game characters, missions, towns, or logos
cropped wash line or cropped back door

self_check: Reads as PS3-era western game art at square page-card scale.
The wash lines, back doors, chickens, ash barrels, workers, and footpaths are readable.
Every visible prop belongs plausibly to 1899 back-lot domestic life.
The image shows work and hidden social routes rather than tidy decoration.
No copied game characters, readable modern text, or inherited non-western symbols.
